steps.title: Reverse the relationship between populations
steps.content:
Given that the 2018 population is 1.27 times the 2014 population, I need to reverse this to express 2014 in terms of 2018.

If $Z_{2018} = 1.27 \cdot Z_{2014}$, then dividing both sides by 1.27:
$$Z_{2014} = \frac{1}{1.27} \cdot Z_{2018}$$

steps.title: Set up the percentage equation
steps.content:
The problem asks for $p$ such that 2014 is $p\%$ of 2018:
$$Z_{2014} = \frac{p}{100} \cdot Z_{2018}$$
Comparing with the equation from Step 1, the coefficient of $Z_{2018}$ must match:
$$\frac{p}{100} = \frac{1}{1.27}$$

steps.title: Solve for p and round to nearest integer
steps.content:
Multiplying both sides by 100:
$$p = \frac{100}{1.27}$$
Calculating: $100 \div 1.27 \approx 78.74$

Rounding to the nearest whole number, $p = 79$.
